contactInformation.residentialAddress: 67-76 Booth St, Forest Hills, New York 11375

results.possibleIdentity: George Newfield was an engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s who resided in Forest Hills, New York. He worked for Consolidated Avionics Corporation, a company based in Westbury, Long Island (near Forest Hills), which specialized in transistorized power supplies, aerospace power converters for rockets and missiles, and microminiature electronics for space applications. Newfield was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. He was listed in the E.A.T. directory as a technical expert available to assist artists, with specific expertise in microminiature electronics and power supplies for space systems. He was likely related to Marcia Newfield, a researcher at Bell Laboratories who was also active in E.A.T. projects during the same period, such as the 'Children and Communication' project for the 'Projects Outside Art' exhibition. George Newfield later remained active in the New York area community, including long-term membership in the Beth El Synagogue in New Rochelle.
